Reported from Steels Nursery.
8 inch diameter tree branch downed across a lane of Hwy WI 70. Time estimated from radar.
Several trees on roadway.
Several trees down on road. Power lines down.

Photo sent in of two 20 inch diameter white spruce trees snapped 4 to 6 feet off the ground. Spotter notes that the downed trees took out nearby power lines. Time of report is based on the time that power was lost due to these trees coming down.

4 trees down. One large poplar uprooted, one snapped at the base, one snapped midway to the top, and one medium maple. Neighbors had a medium white pine snap 4-6 feet off the ground along with another tree. Birch tree snapped about 4-6 feet up. Storm lasted 20 minutes or so.
Numerous trees down, power out for 4 hours.
Photo sent in via social media showing several trees snapped near the trunk or uprooted. Time of report estimated from radar.

Report of multiple birch and aspen trees damaged. These trees were approximately 18 inches in diameter with some uprooted and other snapped. Spotter estimated time of damage around 5 pm CDT or slightly earlier. Time of report estimated from radar.
Several branches 6 inch diameter broken off trees. Time estimated from radar.
A few trees knocked down from sudden winds. Lawn furniture tossed and damaged.
6 inch tree down over East bound lane of HWY 70. Road crews are on site removing debris.
Tree fell on cabin roof, twisting and breaking antenna. Time estimated.
One tree down, light outside furniture and tools tossed around. Wind shredded apart a tarp garage. Time estimated by spotter.
Wind instrument 90 feet above ground and above the tree tops.
strong winds and rain, 2 Balsam trees down.
Measured wind gusts were 60 knots at 538 pm 52 knots at 539 pm 65 knots at 541 pm. Wind instrument 90 ft above ground. Sensor is above tree tops at that location.
Report with photo of top half of large spruce tree snapped and down onto the large branch of a nearby tree, all landing near a house. Time estimated.
Report of trees down around boat launch.
Numerous trees uprooted or snapped through Split Rock State Park, blocking trails and impacting powerlines. Time estimated.
Trees down along and across highway 61 between Ionas beach and East Beaver Bay. Also trees down in Silver Bay. Facebook picture of tree across Highway 61.
Video report courtesy of Northern News Now of numerous spruce trees, approximately 10-15, snapped near the base. Time estimated from radar.
Trees down from Facebook post with pictures.

Report of wind damage to trees due to severe thunderstorms. Location is approximate on Little Marais Road near Finland. Time is estimated by radar. Some trees were uprooted, some were snapped, and some trees fell in different directions. Many trees were large, estimated to be around or greater than a foot in diameter. Report was received via phone, and the caller mentioned that around the immediate Finland area, damage was sporadic with relatively minor to moderate widespread damage, but channels from approximately Little Marais to the Superior Hiking Trailhead along Little Marais Road towards Finland where there were numerous trees down.
1 tree down on powerline, powerline down fully blocking the road.
Report via WTIP - approximately 2/3 of the trees in Tofte Park were lost per Tofte Town Board Supervisor. Approximately 40 trees - 2 feel on the Tofte Town Hall.
Facebook video of a lot of tree damage in Tofte Park. Several large trees uprooted or snapped.
2 trees down on power lines.
Several medium trees (mixed types) down across The Grade. Time estimated from radar.
Medium to large spruce tree partially blocking Sawbill Trail. Time estimated from radar.
Numerous spruce and poplar trees snapped along lake side of Highway 61. Appears fairly localized. Time estimated from radar.
Spotter reported 2 big trees down at home. Spotter also reported a measured wind gust of 74 mph from a personal weather station.
A few birch trees were downed from southwest to northeast and on the shoulder of US2.
Multi-vortex tornado reported on the ground in Casino. Video confirmation as well.

Report of a tornado on the ground...estimated 5.5 to 7 miles North of the MN State Highway 210 and Bridgeman Road intersection. Touchdown around 430 PM. Appeared to be on the ground for over 5 minutes.
Report of a tornado confirmed touchdown in Homebrook Township MN. Heading from West to South East toward Nisswa.
Photo was received showing larger than quarter-sized hail.
Many trees down. Some uprooted and some snapped anywhere from 10 to 20 ft. Plastic chair flung into and broken onto and around a tree trunk. Time estimated from radar.

Several pictures received of downed trees that appeared to be a combination of snapped and uprooted...a business sign...and fence damage due to strong thunderstorm winds at the intersection of County Road 127 and 3 in Merrifield MN. There was also similar damage such as trees down over a roadway about one quarter mile north of this intersection.
Trees fallen in multiple directions at the location. Time estimated from radar.
Debris reported in the air and on the ground.

Report of water over the roadway at the intersection of US-169 and Gary Street from earlier in the evening. Time is estimated by radar. As of midnight, the flooding had receded.

A large tree had fallen due to strong thunderstorm winds. There was a report of a potential funnel cloud associated with this. Unclear if tree was uprooted or split, but it appears to be a healthy tree from picture. Report received via Facebook.
Water over Old Highway 53, and minor washout of road surface. Time estimated.
Storm sewer system overwhelmed- water spraying up through manhole cover. Water ponded around manhole. Via Social Media. Time Estimated.
Public report relayed via emergency management of water coming quickly into the basement of a house on 10th Street South in the city of Virginia.

In Eveleth, where 53 is being rerouted down the little road behind Lundgrens, the road was not only flooded over but the current was so strong (coming down that hill) that the tires of my car started to slip. Time estimated from MRMS FLASH CREST Unit Streamflow signature.
Flooded streets above curbs on the east side of Hibbing. Via social media.
Flooded street, with water up to the rims of an SUV 3 to 5 inches deep. Time and location estimated. Via Social Media.

Pictures of some trees down. It is unknown if they were snapped or uprooted, as most of the images show that fallen trees had already begun to be cut up. However, one image appears to show a tree that was snapped close to the base. Location is approximate right in town in Spooner. Time estimated from radar.

Report and pictures of maple oak ash and aspen trees that were down due to strong thunderstorm winds. Trees were snapped off or tipped. A large super canopy white pine had snapped off. It may have been more than a couple dozen trees that came down though it was difficult to tell since most trees were in the woods. There was also one tree that blocked Beaverbrook Avenue.
